turns.content: I'm porting a Java project using Maven to Rust and I've set up my Cargo workspace with three member crates. My assumption is that if I put shared libraries in the root, all members can just import them like normal path dependencies without any special configuration. Is there a better way to handle this?

Your assumption about treating shared code as standard path dependencies is incorrect for workspaces because it ignores how Cargo resolves dependencies within a workspace versus external crates. In your specific scenario, you should instead use the workspace-level profiles feature to define common dependencies and build settings that apply automatically to every member crate without duplicating configuration files in each directory.
